The #1 thing getting in my way right now is exercising the right level of discernment in the acquisition process. I’m deeply aware that buying the wrong business isn’t just a bad deal—it’s an unnecessary drain on time, energy, and focus.  Earlier in my career, I had the capacity to fix broken systems and clean up messy operations. In this season, as a toddler mom and caretaker for my 83-year-old mother, that’s no longer the tradeoff I’m willing to make. I’m not looking to rescue a business—I’m looking to acquire one that is already efficient, operationally sound, and built to scale.  Any nervousness I feel isn’t about avoiding action—it’s about being intentional. I want to vet deals thoroughly, avoid being “sold” into a headache, and ensure the business I acquire is one where my strengths and relationships—particularly my Rolodex in entertainment and media—create immediate leverage. I’m comfortable moving forward with courage, even when it feels uncomfortable, as long as the fundamentals are right.
